atoms.docstring_summary: Converts rigid frames and torsion angles (dihedral angles between four consecutive atoms that define the protein backbone geometry) into full 3D coordinates.
Updates 3D rigid frames using predicted gradients.
Equivariant (i.e., the output transforms consistently under rotations and translations of the input) attention mechanism over structured 3D point sets.
Return atoms reached by recursively walking an hPDB-style hierarchy.
review_limitations: No PDB text parser is included; callers must provide in-memory hierarchy objects.
risk_reasons: Scope is limited to the local hPDB ingest hierarchy model rather than full PDB parsing.

atoms.docstring_summary: Return residues reached by recursively walking an hPDB-style hierarchy.
review_limitations: No PDB text parser is included; callers must provide in-memory hierarchy objects.
risk_reasons: Scope is limited to the local hPDB ingest hierarchy model rather than full PDB parsing.

atoms.docstring_summary: Apply average-product correction (APC) over the final two axes.
atoms.description: Apply average-product correction over the final two axes.
parameters: x: np.ndarray with at least two dimensions
atoms.provenance: Matched to upstream mint/modules.py:32 apc implementation at commit 79a9956b80e221066a3007bb156e8e986cba6190.

atoms.docstring_summary: Compute MINT row attention for an alignment tensor using supplied projection weights.
review_limitations: The atom is a stateless replay of the upstream nn.Module and requires callers to pass projection weights and biases explicitly.
self_attn_mask remains unsupported because upstream MINT RowSelfAttention raises NotImplementedError for that argument.
risk_reasons: Torch is required, and projection state is explicit rather than owned by an nn.Module instance.
self_attn_mask is intentionally unsupported to match upstream behavior.
atoms.description: Replay MINT RowSelfAttention over a 4D MSA tensor using explicit q/k/v/out projection parameters.
parameters: x: torch.Tensor shaped (num_rows, num_cols, batch_size, embed_dim)
self_attn_mask: torch.Tensor | None (unsupported by upstream and raises NotImplementedError when supplied)
self_attn_padding_mask: torch.Tensor | None shaped (batch_size, num_rows, num_cols)
io_specs: returns attention probabilities shaped (num_heads, batch_size, num_cols, num_cols)
atoms.provenance: Positive remediation audit against upstream mint/axial_attention.py:12 RowSelfAttention at commit 79a9956b80e221066a3007bb156e8e986cba6190; focused tests compare projection, padding-mask, and no-grad batched-forward behavior to the source class.

atoms.docstring_summary: Compatibility entrypoint for the same MINT row attention calculation.
review_limitations: This compatibility atom intentionally changes the old NumPy approximation into the upstream Torch RowSelfAttention contract because MINT has no NumPy RowSelfAttention implementation.
The atom is a stateless replay of the upstream nn.Module and requires callers to pass projection weights and biases explicitly.
self_attn_mask remains unsupported because upstream MINT RowSelfAttention raises NotImplementedError for that argument.
risk_reasons: The legacy lowercase FQDN no longer exposes a NumPy approximation because upstream MINT only defines Torch RowSelfAttention.
self_attn_mask is intentionally unsupported to match upstream behavior.
atoms.description: Backward-compatible lowercase atom exposing the same MINT RowSelfAttention replay contract.
parameters: x: torch.Tensor shaped (num_rows, num_cols, batch_size, embed_dim)
self_attn_mask: torch.Tensor | None (unsupported by upstream and raises NotImplementedError when supplied)
self_attn_padding_mask: torch.Tensor | None shaped (batch_size, num_rows, num_cols)
io_specs: returns attention probabilities shaped (num_heads, batch_size, num_cols, num_cols)
atoms.provenance: Positive remediation audit against upstream mint/axial_attention.py:12 RowSelfAttention at commit 79a9956b80e221066a3007bb156e8e986cba6190; focused tests verify parity through the lowercase compatibility entrypoint.

atoms.docstring_summary: Encode TCR CDR3/epitope distance matrices and their valid-value mask.
atoms.description: Encode TCR CDR3/epitope distance matrices and validity masks.
io_specs: returns tuple[encoding float32, masking bool] shaped (len(mat_list), max_cdr3, max_epi)
atoms.provenance: Matched to upstream downstream/TCR-Epitope/tcr-interface/teim_utils.py:309 encoding_dist_mat at commit 79a9956b80e221066a3007bb156e8e986cba6190.

atoms.docstring_summary: Retrieve one labeled sequence by index.
atoms.description: Retrieve the label and sequence string at a valid dataset index from aligned dataset state lists.

parameters.name: dataset_state
parameters.description: Dataset state containing aligned sequence_labels and sequence_strs lists.

parameters.name: idx
parameters.description: Python list index of the sequence record to return.

io_specs.name: dataset_state
io_specs.constraints: Must contain sequence_labels and sequence_strs with equal length.

io_specs.name: idx
io_specs.constraints: Valid Python list index for the dataset.

atoms.docstring_summary: Return the number of labeled sequences in dataset_state.
atoms.description: Return the number of sequence labels in the dataset state.

parameters.name: dataset_state
parameters.description: Dataset state containing a sequence_labels list.

atoms.docstring_summary: Build a sequence dataset state snapshot from aligned in-memory inputs.
atoms.description: Copy aligned sequence label and sequence string lists into an explicit dataset state dictionary.

parameters.name: sequence_labels
parameters.description: Sequence identifiers; length must match sequence_strs.

parameters.name: sequence_strs
parameters.description: Biological sequence strings; one sequence per label.

io_specs.name: sequence_labels
io_specs.constraints: Sequence identifiers; length must match sequence_strs.

io_specs.name: sequence_strs
io_specs.constraints: Biological sequence strings; one sequence per label.

io_specs.name: dataset_state
io_specs.constraints: Dictionary with copied sequence_labels and sequence_strs lists.

atoms.docstring_summary: Plan batches under a token budget.
atoms.description: Sort sequences by length and greedily group original dataset indices under a token-budget batching rule.

parameters.name: dataset_state
parameters.description: Dataset state whose sequence_strs lengths drive batching.

parameters.name: toks_per_batch
parameters.description: Maximum token budget used by the greedy batching check.

parameters.name: extra_toks_per_seq
parameters.description: Per-sequence overhead added to each sequence length.

io_specs.name: toks_per_batch
io_specs.constraints: Maximum token budget used by the greedy batching check.

io_specs.name: extra_toks_per_seq
io_specs.constraints: Per-sequence overhead added to each sequence length.

io_specs.name: batch_indices
io_specs.constraints: Each inner list contains original dataset indices.

atoms.docstring_summary: Decorate a class with Fairseq/MINT incremental-state behavior.
review_limitations: Like upstream MINT, dynamic __bases__ mutation is validated for nn.Module-style classes and may not work for arbitrary built-in/layout-incompatible classes.
atoms.description: Decorate an nn.Module-style class with MINT/Fairseq incremental-state behavior.
io_specs: returns the same class object with FairseqIncrementalState prepended to bases
atoms.provenance: Matched to upstream mint/multihead_attention.py FairseqIncrementalState and with_incremental_state at commit 79a9956b80e221066a3007bb156e8e986cba6190.

atoms.docstring_summary: Apply MINT/RoFormer rotary positional embedding to NumPy query/key arrays.

atoms.provenance: Matched to upstream mint/rotary_embedding.py rotate_half/apply_rotary_pos_emb/RotaryEmbedding at commit 79a9956b80e221066a3007bb156e8e986cba6190.

atoms.docstring_summary: Apply MINT/RoFormer rotary positional embedding to torch query/key tensors.
atoms.description: Apply MINT/RoFormer rotate-half rotary embedding to torch query/key tensors.
io_specs: returns tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor] preserving q/k shapes
atoms.provenance: Matched to upstream mint/rotary_embedding.py rotate_half/apply_rotary_pos_emb/RotaryEmbedding at commit 79a9956b80e221066a3007bb156e8e986cba6190.

atoms.docstring_summary: Adds a specialized 'quantum link' between two nodes in a graph, potentially creating a chain of intermediate nodes based on chain_size.
Builds the complementary graph by deriving the inverse edge set relative to the input graph's node set.
Args:
Evaluates whether a candidate left/right feature pair satisfies interaction-distance constraints.
Return the two physically possible interaction-edge layouts for a ligand/receptor feature pair.
Construct immutable algorithm context from constructor inputs so all later stages consume explicit state instead of hidden class fields.
Create initial mapping/unmapping frontier state from a selected starting node.
Orchestrate initialization, greedy extension, and validity checking to produce a greedy Unit Disk (UD) subgraph rooted at the starting node.
Score candidate placements and greedily extend the mapping frontier using ranking and validity filtering flags.
Evaluate whether the current mapping/unmapping pair satisfies graph-lattice consistency constraints.
Advance one D12 greedy-lattice mapping step without mutating the input state.
Collect the graph, lattice, historical subgraphs, and seed for D12 greedy mapping.
Run the D12 greedy expansion loop from ``starting_node`` and validate the final map.
Return a greedy maximum-weight independent-set subgraph mask.
Certify a graph as a unit-disk graph without adding layout-induced edges.
Take the maximum weighted distance and convert it to float as the final bandwidth metric.
Convert the thresholded dense matrix into a Compressed Sparse Row (CSR) representation suitable for graph-based bandwidth reduction.
Compute matrix amplitude from absolute values and construct the truncation sweep used to explore candidate permutations.
Enumerate all entries and compute absolute weighted index distance for each element as abs(value * (row_index - col_index)).
Run reverse Cuthill-McKee in symmetric mode on the sparse matrix and materialize the permutation as a NumPy array.
Create a thresholded copy of the input matrix where all entries below the threshold are set to zero, preserving the original input matrix.
Generate candidate permutations by calling thresholded bandwidth minimization for each scaled truncation value.
Return the accumulated permutation from the terminal state as the function result.
Create the sanitized working matrix and initial optimization state for greedy bandwidth descent.

atoms.return_annotation: tuple[np.ndarray | dict[str, np.ndarray | list[int] | int], list[int], np.ndarray, int]
atoms.docstring_summary: From the current working matrix, compute one candidate permutation and its resulting matrix bandwidth.
Evaluate each candidate by permuting the matrix and computing resulting bandwidth, then return the best permutation as a Python list.
Apply iteration budget checks, compare candidate vs current bandwidth, and either accept the candidate state or terminate without update.
Check that the input matrix has identical row and column counts; fail if not square.
Verify the matrix is numerically symmetric within tolerance before any reordering logic proceeds.
Verify the input matrix is symmetric within tolerance and fail fast if the precondition is violated.
Calculates the total weight of a given list of nodes in the graph.
Checks if the given subset of nodes is an independent set in the graph.
Load graphs from folder.

Convert a Maximum Weight Independent Set (MWIS) problem to a Quadratic Unconstrained Binary Optimization (QUBO) matrix. Encodes node weights as diagonal entries and edge penalties as off-diagonal entries so the problem can be solved by a binary optimizer.

Executes the core quantum simulation loop. It evolves the quantum register through an adiabatic sequence to find low-energy states of the problem Hamiltonian, effectively sampling from the solution distribution.
review_limitations: Requires the documented Pulser/emulator optional dependency group for runtime quantum backend execution.

atoms.return_annotation: tuple[QuantumRegister, dict[str, object], list[Permutation], dict[str, bool], Integer]
atoms.docstring_summary: Initializes the quantum annealing problem. It computes simulation parameters like the minimum and maximum coupling strengths (u) based on the graph and register coordinates, and prepares the initial state for the solver.
review_limitations: Requires the documented Pulser/emulator optional dependency group for runtime quantum backend execution.

atoms.docstring_summary: Processes the raw results from the quantum sampler. It interprets the measurement count distribution to extract the top solutions for the optimization problem.
review_limitations: Requires the documented Pulser/emulator optional dependency group for runtime quantum backend execution.

atoms.docstring_summary: Build the time-dependent adiabatic pulse sequence for the neutral-atom device.
review_limitations: Requires the documented Pulser/emulator optional dependency group for runtime quantum backend execution.

atoms.docstring_summary: Compute the min and max interaction energy bounds across all atom pairs.
review_limitations: Requires the documented Pulser/emulator optional dependency group for runtime quantum backend execution.

atoms.docstring_summary: Run the adiabatic pulse sequence on the chosen quantum backend and return bitstring counts.
review_limitations: Requires the documented Pulser/emulator optional dependency group for runtime quantum backend execution.

atoms.docstring_summary: Post-processes the raw measurement count distribution to decode, rank, and filter the top-k bitstring solutions.
review_limitations: Requires the documented Pulser/emulator optional dependency group for runtime quantum backend execution.

atoms.docstring_summary: Solve the Maximum Weight Independent Set (MWIS) problem end-to-end on a neutral-atom quantum device.
review_limitations: Requires the documented Pulser/emulator optional dependency group for runtime quantum backend execution.
